3d cross section silhouette challenge.name: 3D Cross Section Silhouette Challenge
3d cross section silhouette challenge.description:
Overview
3D Cross Section Silhouette Challenge is a 3D-plus-equations reasoning benchmark. Each task is a single 832x832 PNG depicting a geometric solid sliced by a flat cutting plane, with six candidate 2D silhouettes labelled A, B, C, D, E, F shown underneath. Exactly one of the six silhouettes is the true plane-solid cross-section, and the task is to pick that letter.

Twelve solid families are used across the dataset: sphere, ellipsoid, rectangular box, capped cylinder, elliptic paraboloid, triangular prism, capped cone, torus, square pyramid, one-sheet hyperboloid, bicone, and L-shaped prism. Six of them (sphere, ellipsoid, box, cylinder, paraboloid, triangular prism) are in-distribution and appear in training and the in-distribution test split. The other six (cone, torus, square pyramid, hyperboloid, bicone, L-shaped prism) are held out and appear only in the test_novel_solids and test_hard diagnostic splits.

Each solid is rendered at a random 3D orientation (sphere only, which is rotation-invariant, is kept axis-aligned). The cutting plane is always axis-aligned in the world frame: x = k, y = k, or z = k with an integer k. World-frame x, y, z axes pass through the origin; only the axis the plane is aligned with carries integer tick marks and numeric labels, and the plane's own position on that axis is highlighted as a larger orange tick plus a bold number. So the solver reads the plane position directly off the labelled axis.

The printed solid equation is in the solid's local reference frame. The printed plane equation is in the world reference frame. To find the cross-section, the solver must combine the solid's shape (from the equation), its orientation (visible in the 3D render), and the plane's position (from the plane equation and the highlighted axis tick).

Every silhouette is canonicalised with its major axis horizontal. Matching is therefore about the shape of the cross-section, not its orientation in the plane's local frame. The six options span different coarse shape categories (circle, ellipse variants, polygon variants, triangle, annulus, disjoint pair, non-convex), so the tiles are visually distinct at a glance and the correct letter is the one whose shape the cross-section actually has.

The dataset carries 500 raw tasks total, each with a globally-unique (solid_equation, plane_equation) pair. No two tasks share a symbolic configuration.

Challenge Characteristics
Domain: synthetic, procedurally generated 3D-geometry puzzles
Input modality: a single 832x832 RGB PNG per task
Output modality: a single uppercase letter in {A, B, C, D, E, F}
Training data: 100 tasks with images and ground-truth letters
Test data: 400 held-out tasks across three diagnostic splits
Evaluation: negative-marking accuracy, +1.0 correct, -0.25 wrong, 0.0 skip, averaged across the test set
Output vocabulary: 6 answer tokens plus a skip token (empty string, whitespace, or -)
Dataset
Every task is delivered as an 832x832 RGB image with the following layout:

+------------------------------------------------+  
|                                                |  
|          [ 3D isometric render ]               |  
|   solid (blue) at random orientation +         |  
|   cutting plane (light orange, dotted grid)    |  
|   + x / y / z axes, numbered ticks on the      |  
|   plane's axis, orange tick at the plane's k   |  
|                                                |  
|  +------------------------------------------+  |  
|  | Solid (name):  implicit equation         |  |  
|  | Plane:         x = k   /   y = k   /     |  |  
|  |                z = k                     |  |  
|  +------------------------------------------+  |  
|                                                |  
|        Which silhouette is the cross-section?  |  
|   +--------+ +--------+ +--------+             |  
|   |   A    | |   B    | |   C    |             |  
|   +--------+ +--------+ +--------+             |  
|   +--------+ +--------+ +--------+             |  
|   |   D    | |   E    | |   F    |             |  
|   +--------+ +--------+ +--------+             |  
+------------------------------------------------+  
The solid is rendered as a smoothly anti-aliased wireframe with a pastel-blue fill and a dark outline.
The cutting plane is a light semi-transparent orange rectangle with a faint dotted grid overlay, drawn so it passes through the solid without dominating the scene.
The world x axis is drawn in a red tint, y in green, z in blue. Only the axis matched to the cutting plane carries numbered integer tick marks; the plane's own position on that axis is a larger orange tick with a bold number.
The printed solid equation uses Unicode math (superscript 2, square root, set-inclusion).
Every plane equation is of the form x = k, y = k, or z = k with integer k.
Silhouette tiles are smoothly filled pastel-blue shapes with dark outlines, scaled to the same canonical bounding box. Every silhouette has been canonicalised so its major axis lies horizontal.
A single letter (A through F) is the ground-truth answer; exactly one of the six candidates matches the cross-section.
Data Files
File	Description	Columns
train.csv	100 training tasks	id, image, answer
train_images/	100 task PNGs (832x832)	
test.csv	400 held-out test tasks	id, image
test_images/	400 test PNGs	
sample_submission.csv	Submission format template	id, answer
The image column is a relative path of the form train_images/train_0000000.png. Participants may carve their own validation split out of train.csv.

The test set is a shuffled mixture of three diagnostic splits:

test_iid (100 tasks): in-distribution baseline over the six in-distribution solid families.
test_novel_solids (150 tasks): held-out solid families (cone, torus, square pyramid, hyperboloid, bicone, L-prism).
test_hard (150 tasks): a second held-out-solids sample, disjoint from test_novel_solids.
Split labels are not exposed in the public test CSV.

Example Row (from train.csv)
id:      train_0000000  
image:   train_images/train_0000000.png  
answer:  E  
Submission Format
Submit a CSV with exactly the columns id and answer. One row per test task; the id column must match test.csv exactly.

id,answer  
test_000000,A  
test_000001,F  
test_000002,C  
Formatting requirements

Each answer is either a single uppercase letter in {A, B, C, D, E, F} or a skip token.
A skip token is an empty string, whitespace only, or the literal character -. A skip earns exactly zero on that row.
Whitespace is trimmed and the first alphabetic character is uppercased before comparison, so " c " is treated as C.
Any non-skip value whose normalised first alphabetic character is outside {A, B, C, D, E, F} is treated as a skip (zero) on that row.
Evaluation: Negative-Marking Accuracy
Per-row scoring:

+1.00 if the prediction matches the ground-truth letter exactly  
-0.25 if the prediction normalises to any other valid letter (a wrong guess)  
 0.00 if the prediction is a skip (empty, whitespace, or "-")  
The final benchmark score is the mean per-task score over the test set, in the closed interval [-0.25, +1.0]. A perfect submission returns 1.0; blanket wrong guessing returns -0.25; fully skipping returns 0.0. Uniform random guessing over six letters has expected value 1/6 * 1.0 + 5/6 * (-0.25) ~= -0.042, so any strategy that cannot learn a signal above chance is actively punished for guessing and should prefer to skip. The break-even confidence is p = 0.20.

Method Requirements
CROSSECTION is a learning-centric benchmark. The intended solution space is any learned approach that takes the rendered image as input and produces a letter.

Prohibited approaches

Rule-based or hand-coded solutions that OCR the printed equation, reconstruct the solid and plane in a symbolic computer-algebra system, and compute the intersection analytically. The benchmark measures what a learned visual-plus-symbolic pipeline can acquire, not the author's ability to re-implement the generator.
Hardcoded lookup tables indexed by the exact equation strings or rendered mask hashes.
Test-time fine-tuning, retrieval over the test set, or per-task adaptation using the test labels.
Allowed approaches

Any learned image-to-label pipeline, including end-to-end CNN or ViT classifiers, image-to-sequence models that emit the letter as a single token, two-stage detect-then-classify systems, vision-language models prompted with the image and a natural-language question, or ensembles of any of the above.
Data augmentation (colour jitter, crops within the layout), auxiliary objectives, or multi-head training that predicts the letter alongside other structural labels, provided only the supplied training data is used to fit parameters.
OCR on the printed equations, provided the OCR model itself is either off-the-shelf or trained only on the supplied training data and is not chained to a hand-written symbolic solver.
 

Expected Output
Output to ./working/submission.csv

extra_files.gen_nb.py:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate an L4-friendly notebook for the 3D Cross Section challenge."""

import json


cells = []


def md(src: str) -> None:
    lines = [line + "\n" for line in src.strip("\n").split("\n")]
    if lines:
        lines[-1] = lines[-1].rstrip("\n")
    cells.append(
        {
            "cell_type": "markdown",
            "metadata": {},
            "source": lines,
        }
    )


def code(src: str) -> None:
    lines = [line + "\n" for line in src.strip("\n").split("\n")]
    if lines:
        lines[-1] = lines[-1].rstrip("\n")
    cells.append(
        {
            "cell_type": "code",
            "metadata": {},
            "source": lines,
            "outputs": [],
            "execution_count": None,
        }
    )


md(
    """
# 🎯 3D Cross Section Silhouette Challenge — L4 Notebook

Notebook này được viết lại để chạy ổn trên **GPU L4** và bám sát bản chất bài toán hơn:

- tách riêng **scene**, **solid equation**, **plane equation** và **6 candidate tiles**
- bỏ phần **label A-F** khỏi crop tile để giảm leakage/bias theo vị trí
- dùng **frozen vision backbones** để trích đặc trưng, sau đó học **tile-ranking head**
- tối ưu theo **negative-marking score** và có cơ chế **skip**
- cache feature vào `working/` để rerun nhanh hơn

Đầu ra chính nằm ở `working/submisison.csv` và mirror thêm sang `working/submission.csv`.
"""
)

code(
    """
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# 📁 Prepare Dataset
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
import os
import zipfile
from pathlib import Path

def find_data_root():
    for root in [Path("./dataset/public"), Path("./dataset"), Path(".")]:
        if (root / "train.csv").exists() and (root / "test.csv").exists():
            return root
    return None

data_root = find_data_root()

if data_root is None:
    zip_path = Path("dataset.zip")
    if not zip_path.exists():
        try:
            from google.colab import files
        except Exception as exc:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Không tìm thấy dữ liệu. Hãy upload dataset.zip hoặc mount Drive trước."
            ) from exc

        print("📁 Upload dataset.zip ...")
        uploaded = files.upload()
        if "dataset.zip" not in uploaded:
            raise RuntimeError("Bạn cần upload đúng file dataset.zip")

    print("📦 Extracting dataset.zip ...")
    with zipfile.ZipFile("dataset.zip", "r") as zf:
        zf.extractall(".")
    data_root = find_data_root()

if data_root is None:
    raise RuntimeError("Không locate được train.csv/test.csv sau khi giải nén.")

print(f"✅ Data root: {data_root.resolve()}")
print(f"✅ train.csv exists: {(data_root / 'train.csv').exists()}")
print(f"✅ test.csv exists:  {(data_root / 'test.csv').exists()}")
"""
)

# ══════════════════════════════════════════

from albumentations.pytorch import ToTensorV2
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

if hasattr(torch, "set_float32_matmul_precision"):

class CFG:
    seed = 42

    text_size = 224

    ]

    # 832 x 832 layout
    scene_box = (20, 410, 25, 807)

        (672, 802, 283, 520),  # E

    ]

    # Cut away border + letter badge in the upper-left corner of each tile

CFG.work_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)


def seed_all(seed: int) -> None:

    torch.manual_seed(seed)

    torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic = False

seed_all(CFG.seed)

    """
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# 🔍 Quick EDA + Crop Preview
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
def load_rgb(path):
    img = cv2.imread(str(path))
    if img is None:
        raise FileNotFoundError(path)
    return cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)


def crop_box(img, box):
    y1, y2, x1, x2 = box
    return img[y1:y2, x1:x2]


def extract_tiles(img, inner=True):
    out = []
    for box in CFG.tile_boxes:
        tile = crop_box(img, box)
        if inner:
            m = CFG.tile_inner
            tile = tile[m["top"]: tile.shape[0] - m["bottom"], m["left"]: tile.shape[1] - m["right"]]
        out.append(tile)
    return out


sample_row = train_df.iloc[0]
sample_img = load_rgb(sample_row["image_path"])
scene_crop = crop_box(sample_img, CFG.scene_box)
equation_crop = crop_box(sample_img, CFG.equation_box)
solid_equation_crop = crop_box(sample_img, CFG.solid_equation_box)
plane_equation_crop = crop_box(sample_img, CFG.plane_equation_box)
tile_crops = extract_tiles(sample_img, inner=True)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(16, 8))
axes[0, 0].imshow(sample_img)
axes[0, 0].set_title(f"Full image\\nGT = {sample_row['answer']}")
axes[0, 1].imshow(scene_crop)
axes[0, 1].set_title("Scene crop")
axes[0, 2].imshow(equation_crop)
axes[0, 2].set_title("Equation box")
axes[0, 3].imshow(solid_equation_crop)
axes[0, 3].set_title("Solid equation")

for i in range(4):

    axes[1, i].set_title(f"Tile {chr(65 + i)}")

for ax in axes.ravel():

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(12, 3))
axes[0].imshow(plane_equation_crop); axes[0].set_title("Plane equation")
axes[1].imshow(tile_crops[4]); axes[1].set_title("Tile E")
axes[2].imshow(tile_crops[5]); axes[2].set_title("Tile F")
for ax in axes:
    ax.axis("off")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
"""
)


code(
    """
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# 🧰 Region Utilities
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
def build_transform(size=224):
    return A.Compose(
        [
            A.LongestMaxSize(max_size=size),
            A.PadIfNeeded(
                min_height=size,
                min_width=size,
                border_mode=cv2.BORDER_CONSTANT,
                value=(255, 255, 255),
            ),
            A.Normalize(mean=(0.485, 0.456, 0.406), std=(0.229, 0.224, 0.225)),
            ToTensorV2(),
        ]
    )


def build_text_transform(size=224):
    return A.Compose(
        [
            A.Resize(height=size, width=size),
            A.Normalize(mean=(0.485, 0.456, 0.406), std=(0.229, 0.224, 0.225)),
            ToTensorV2(),
        ]
    )


def tile_mask(tile):
    hsv = cv2.cvtColor(tile, cv2.COLOR_RGB2HSV)
    sat = hsv[..., 1].astype(np.float32)
    val = hsv[..., 2].astype(np.float32)
    mask = ((sat > 18) & (val < 252)).astype(np.uint8)
    kernel3 = np.ones((3, 3), np.uint8)
    kernel5 = np.ones((5, 5), np.uint8)
    mask = cv2.morphologyEx(mask, cv2.MORPH_OPEN, kernel3)
    mask = cv2.morphologyEx(mask, cv2.MORPH_CLOSE, kernel5)
    return mask


def contour_holes(mask):
    contours, hierarchy = cv2.findContours(mask, cv2.RETR_CCOMP, cv2.CHAIN_APPROX_SIMPLE)
    if hierarchy is None:
        return 0
    hierarchy = hierarchy[0]
    holes = 0
    for i in range(len(contours)):
        if hierarchy[i][3] != -1:
            holes += 1
    return holes


def tile_geom_features(tile):
    mask = tile_mask(tile)
    h, w = mask.shape
    area = float(mask.sum())
    area_ratio = area / (h * w + 1e-6)

    ys, xs = np.where(mask > 0)
    if len(xs) == 0:
        return np.zeros(12, dtype=np.float32)

    x1, x2 = xs.min(), xs.max()
    y1, y2 = ys.min(), ys.max()
    bw = float(max(1, x2 - x1 + 1))
    bh = float(max(1, y2 - y1 + 1))
    bbox_area = bw * bh
    aspect = bw / bh
    extent = area / (bbox_area + 1e-6)

    contours, _ = cv2.findContours(mask, cv2.RETR_EXTERNAL, cv2.CHAIN_APPROX_SIMPLE)
    cnt = max(contours, key=cv2.contourArea) if contours else None
    perimeter = float(cv2.arcLength(cnt, True)) if cnt is not None else 0.0
    hull_area = float(cv2.contourArea(cv2.convexHull(cnt))) if cnt is not None else 0.0
    solidity = area / (hull_area + 1e-6) if hull_area > 0 else 0.0
    circularity = 4.0 * math.pi * area / (perimeter * perimeter + 1e-6) if perimeter > 0 else 0.0
    components, _ = cv2.connectedComponents(mask)
    holes = contour_holes(mask)

    norm_x = xs.astype(np.float32) / max(1.0, w - 1)
    norm_y = ys.astype(np.float32) / max(1.0, h - 1)
    cx = float(norm_x.mean())
    cy = float(norm_y.mean())
    sx = float(norm_x.std())
    sy = float(norm_y.std())

    return np.array(
        [
            area_ratio,
            aspect,
            extent,
            solidity,
            circularity,
            float(components - 1),
            float(holes),
            cx,
            cy,
            sx,
            sy,
            perimeter / (2 * (h + w) + 1e-6),
        ],
        dtype=np.float32,
    )


def confidence_from_probs(probs):
    sorted_probs = np.sort(probs, axis=1)
    top1 = sorted_probs[:, -1]
    top2 = sorted_probs[:, -2]
    margin = top1 - top2
    entropy = -(probs * np.log(probs + 1e-12)).sum(axis=1) / math.log(probs.shape[1])
    return 0.60 * top1 + 0.25 * margin + 0.15 * (1.0 - entropy)


def neg_mark_score(preds, labels, confs, thr):
    score = 0.0
    for pred, label, conf in zip(preds, labels, confs):
        if conf < thr:
            score += 0.0
        elif pred == label:
            score += 1.0
        else:
            score -= 0.25
    return score / len(preds)


def sweep_threshold(probs, labels, low=0.15, high=0.80, step=0.005):
    preds = probs.argmax(1)
    confs = confidence_from_probs(probs)
    best_thr, best_nm = low, -1e9
    for thr in np.arange(low, high + 1e-9, step):
        nm = neg_mark_score(preds, labels, confs, thr)
        if nm > best_nm:
            best_nm = nm
            best_thr = float(thr)
    return best_thr, float(best_nm), confs


def autocast_context():
    if CFG.use_amp:
        return torch.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=torch.float16)
    return nullcontext()


print("✅ Utility functions ready")
"""
)

# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# 🧊 Extract Frozen Region Features + Cache

@torch.no_grad()
def extract_all_features(train_df, test_df):
    if CFG.feature_cache.exists():
        cache = np.load(CFG.feature_cache, allow_pickle=True)
        cached_train_ids = cache.get("train_ids")

                return {
                    "train_context": cache["train_context"],
                    "test_context": cache["test_context"],
                    "train_tiles": cache["train_tiles"],
                    "test_tiles": cache["test_tiles"],
                    "train_extra": cache["train_extra"],
                    "test_extra": cache["test_extra"],
                }
        print("⚠️ Feature cache exists but ids do not match current dataset. Recomputing.")

    vision_transform = build_transform(CFG.region_size)
    text_transform = build_text_transform(CFG.text_size)

    split_outputs = {
        "train_context_parts": [],
        "test_context_parts": [],
        "train_tile_parts": [],
        "test_tile_parts": [],
    }

    train_geom = []
    test_geom = []

    for bname in CFG.backbones:
        print(f"\\n🔧 Backbone: {bname}")
        model = timm.create_model(bname, pretrained=True, num_classes=0).to(CFG.device).eval()
        feat_dim = model.num_features

        for split_name, frame, geom_store in [
            ("train", train_df, train_geom),
            ("test", test_df, test_geom),
        ]:
            context_feat = np.zeros((len(frame), feat_dim * 3), dtype=np.float32)
            tile_feat = np.zeros((len(frame), 6, feat_dim), dtype=np.float32)

            if len(geom_store) == 0:
                geom_feat = np.zeros((len(frame), 6, 12), dtype=np.float32)
            else:
                geom_feat = geom_store[0]

            for idx, row in enumerate(frame.itertuples(index=False)):
                img = load_rgb(row.image_path)
                scene = crop_box(img, CFG.scene_box)
                solid_equation = crop_box(img, CFG.solid_equation_box)
                plane_equation = crop_box(img, CFG.plane_equation_box)
                tiles = extract_tiles(img, inner=True)

                crops = [
                    vision_transform(image=scene)["image"],
                    text_transform(image=solid_equation)["image"],
                    text_transform(image=plane_equation)["image"],
                ]
                crops.extend(vision_transform(image=tile)["image"] for tile in tiles)
                batch = torch.stack(crops).to(CFG.device)

                with autocast_context():
                    feats = model(batch).detach().float().cpu().numpy()

                context_feat[idx] = np.concatenate([feats[0], feats[1], feats[2]], axis=0)
                tile_feat[idx] = feats[3:]

                if len(geom_store) == 0:
                    for tile_idx, tile in enumerate(tiles):
                        geom_feat[idx, tile_idx] = tile_geom_features(tile)

            if split_name == "train":
                split_outputs["train_context_parts"].append(context_feat)
                split_outputs["train_tile_parts"].append(tile_feat)
            else:
                split_outputs["test_context_parts"].append(context_feat)
                split_outputs["test_tile_parts"].append(tile_feat)

            if len(geom_store) == 0:
                geom_store.append(geom_feat)

        del model
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
        gc.collect()

    train_context = np.concatenate(split_outputs["train_context_parts"], axis=1)
    test_context = np.concatenate(split_outputs["test_context_parts"], axis=1)
    train_tiles = np.concatenate(split_outputs["train_tile_parts"], axis=2)
    test_tiles = np.concatenate(split_outputs["test_tile_parts"], axis=2)

    train_geom = train_geom[0]
    test_geom = test_geom[0]

def build_extras(context_arr, tile_arr, geom_arr):
    ctx_dim = context_arr.shape[1] // 3
    scene_feat = context_arr[:, :ctx_dim]
    solid_feat = context_arr[:, ctx_dim : 2 * ctx_dim]
    plane_feat = context_arr[:, 2 * ctx_dim :]

    def normalize(x, axis=-1):
        norm = np.linalg.norm(x, axis=axis, keepdims=True) + 1e-6
        return x / norm

    scene_n = normalize(scene_feat)
    solid_n = normalize(solid_feat)
    plane_n = normalize(plane_feat)
    tiles_n = normalize(tile_arr, axis=2)

    scene_cos = np.sum(tiles_n * scene_n[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    solid_cos = np.sum(tiles_n * solid_n[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    plane_cos = np.sum(tiles_n * plane_n[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    scene_l2 = np.linalg.norm(tile_arr - scene_feat[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    solid_l2 = np.linalg.norm(tile_arr - solid_feat[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    plane_l2 = np.linalg.norm(tile_arr - plane_feat[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)

    scene_solid_cos = np.sum(scene_n * solid_n, axis=1, keepdims=True)[:, None, :]
    scene_plane_cos = np.sum(scene_n * plane_n, axis=1, keepdims=True)[:, None, :]
    solid_plane_cos = np.sum(solid_n * plane_n, axis=1, keepdims=True)[:, None, :]
    scene_solid_cos = np.repeat(scene_solid_cos, 6, axis=1)
    scene_plane_cos = np.repeat(scene_plane_cos, 6, axis=1)
    solid_plane_cos = np.repeat(solid_plane_cos, 6, axis=1)

    tile_sim = np.matmul(tiles_n, np.transpose(tiles_n, (0, 2, 1)))
        mean_other = []
        max_other = []
        min_other = []
        for i in range(6):
            others = [j for j in range(6) if j != i]
            mean_other.append(tile_sim[:, i, others].mean(axis=1, keepdims=True))
            max_other.append(tile_sim[:, i, others].max(axis=1, keepdims=True))
            min_other.append(tile_sim[:, i, others].min(axis=1, keepdims=True))

        mean_other = np.stack(mean_other, axis=1)
        max_other = np.stack(max_other, axis=1)
        min_other = np.stack(min_other, axis=1)

        return np.concatenate(
        [
            scene_cos,
            solid_cos,
            plane_cos,
            scene_l2,
            solid_l2,
            plane_l2,
            scene_solid_cos,
            scene_plane_cos,
            solid_plane_cos,
            mean_other,
            max_other,
            min_other,
                geom_arr,
            ],
            axis=2,
        ).astype(np.float32)

    train_extra = build_extras(train_context, train_tiles, train_geom)
    test_extra = build_extras(test_context, test_tiles, test_geom)

    np.savez_compressed(
        CFG.feature_cache,
        train_ids=train_df["id"].values,
        test_ids=test_df["id"].values,
        train_context=train_context,
        test_context=test_context,
        train_tiles=train_tiles,
        test_tiles=test_tiles,
        train_extra=train_extra,
        test_extra=test_extra,
    )
    print(f"💾 Saved feature cache to {CFG.feature_cache}")

    return {
        "train_context": train_context,
        "test_context": test_context,
        "train_tiles": train_tiles,
        "test_tiles": test_tiles,
        "train_extra": train_extra,
        "test_extra": test_extra,
}


feature_dict = extract_all_features(train_df, test_df)
train_context = feature_dict["train_context"]
test_context = feature_dict["test_context"]
train_tiles = feature_dict["train_tiles"]
test_tiles = feature_dict["test_tiles"]

test_extra = feature_dict["test_extra"]

print("test_tiles:   ", test_tiles.shape)
print("train_extra:  ", train_extra.shape)
print("test_extra:   ", test_extra.shape)
"""

code(
    """
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# 🧠 Dataset + Tile Ranking Model
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
def fit_standardizer(ctx, tiles, extra):
    tile_flat = tiles.reshape(-1, tiles.shape[-1])
    extra_flat = extra.reshape(-1, extra.shape[-1])
    return {
        "ctx_mean": ctx.mean(axis=0),
        "ctx_std": ctx.std(axis=0) + 1e-6,
        "tile_mean": tile_flat.mean(axis=0),
        "tile_std": tile_flat.std(axis=0) + 1e-6,
        "extra_mean": extra_flat.mean(axis=0),
        "extra_std": extra_flat.std(axis=0) + 1e-6,
    }


def apply_standardizer(ctx, tiles, extra, stats):
    ctx = (ctx - stats["ctx_mean"]) / stats["ctx_std"]
    tiles = (tiles - stats["tile_mean"]) / stats["tile_std"]
    extra = (extra - stats["extra_mean"]) / stats["extra_std"]
    return ctx.astype(np.float32), tiles.astype(np.float32), extra.astype(np.float32)


class RankingDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, ctx, tiles, extra, labels=None):
        self.ctx = torch.tensor(ctx, dtype=torch.float32)
        self.tiles = torch.tensor(tiles, dtype=torch.float32)
        self.extra = torch.tensor(extra, dtype=torch.float32)
        self.labels = None if labels is None else torch.tensor(labels, dtype=torch.long)

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.ctx)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        item = {
            "ctx": self.ctx[idx],
            "tiles": self.tiles[idx],
            "extra": self.extra[idx],
        }
        if self.labels is not None:
            item["label"] = self.labels[idx]
        return item


class TileRanker(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, ctx_dim, tile_dim, extra_dim, hidden=256, dropout=0.25):
        super().__init__()
        self.ctx_proj = nn.Sequential(
            nn.LayerNorm(ctx_dim),
            nn.Linear(ctx_dim, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
        )
        self.tile_proj = nn.Sequential(
            nn.LayerNorm(tile_dim),
            nn.Linear(tile_dim, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
        )
        self.extra_proj = nn.Sequential(
            nn.LayerNorm(extra_dim),
            nn.Linear(extra_dim, hidden // 2),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout * 0.5),
        )
        self.scorer = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(hidden * 4 + hidden // 2, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden, 96),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout * 0.5),
            nn.Linear(96, 1),
        )

    def forward(self, ctx, tiles, extra):
        ctx_h = self.ctx_proj(ctx)
        tile_h = self.tile_proj(tiles)
        extra_h = self.extra_proj(extra)

        ctx_r = ctx_h.unsqueeze(1).expand(-1, tiles.size(1), -1)
        pair = torch.cat(
            [
                ctx_r,
                tile_h,
                torch.abs(ctx_r - tile_h),
                ctx_r * tile_h,
                extra_h,
            ],
            dim=-1,
        )
        logits = self.scorer(pair).squeeze(-1)
        return logits


print("✅ Ranking dataset and model ready")
"""
)


code(
    """
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
# 🚀 5-Fold CV + Seed Averaging
# ══════════════════════════════════════════
ctx_dim = train_context.shape[1]
tile_dim = train_tiles.shape[2]
extra_dim = train_extra.shape[2]

oof_probs = np.zeros((len(train_df), 6), dtype=np.float32)
test_probs_all = []
fold_records = []

for seed in CFG.seeds:
    print(f"\\n{'#' * 68}")
    print(f"SEED = {seed}")
    print(f"{'#' * 68}")
    seed_all(seed)

    skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=CFG.n_folds, shuffle=True, random_state=seed)

    for fold, (tr_idx, va_idx) in enumerate(skf.split(train_df, train_df["answer"]), start=1):
        print(f"\\n--- Fold {fold}/{CFG.n_folds} ---")

        stats = fit_standardizer(
            train_context[tr_idx],
            train_tiles[tr_idx],
            train_extra[tr_idx],
        )

        tr_ctx, tr_tiles, tr_extra = apply_standardizer(
            train_context[tr_idx],
            train_tiles[tr_idx],
            train_extra[tr_idx],
            stats,
        )
        va_ctx, va_tiles, va_extra = apply_standardizer(
            train_context[va_idx],
            train_tiles[va_idx],
            train_extra[va_idx],
            stats,
        )
        te_ctx, te_tiles, te_extra = apply_standardizer(
            test_context,
            test_tiles,
            test_extra,
            stats,
        )

        tr_ds = RankingDataset(tr_ctx, tr_tiles, tr_extra, train_labels[tr_idx])
        va_ds = RankingDataset(va_ctx, va_tiles, va_extra, train_labels[va_idx])
        te_ds = RankingDataset(te_ctx, te_tiles, te_extra, None)

        tr_loader = DataLoader(
            tr_ds,
            batch_size=CFG.batch_size,
            shuffle=True,
            num_workers=CFG.num_workers,
            pin_memory=torch.cuda.is_available(),
        )
        va_loader = DataLoader(
            va_ds,
            batch_size=CFG.batch_size,
            shuffle=False,
            num_workers=CFG.num_workers,
            pin_memory=torch.cuda.is_available(),
        )
        te_loader = DataLoader(
            te_ds,
            batch_size=CFG.batch_size,
            shuffle=False,
            num_workers=CFG.num_workers,
            pin_memory=torch.cuda.is_available(),
        )

        model = TileRanker(ctx_dim, tile_dim, extra_dim).to(CFG.device)
        criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=CFG.label_smoothing)
        optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=CFG.lr, weight_decay=CFG.wd)
        scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=CFG.epochs)

        best_metric = -1e9
        best_state = None
        best_thr = 0.20
        best_acc = 0.0
        patience_left = CFG.patience

        for epoch in range(1, CFG.epochs + 1):
            model.train()
            train_loss = 0.0

            for batch in tr_loader:
                ctx = batch["ctx"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                tiles = batch["tiles"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                extra = batch["extra"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                labels = batch["label"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)

                if CFG.feature_noise > 0:
                    ctx = ctx + torch.randn_like(ctx) * CFG.feature_noise
                    tiles = tiles + torch.randn_like(tiles) * CFG.feature_noise
                    extra = extra + torch.randn_like(extra) * (CFG.feature_noise * 0.5)

                optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
                logits = model(ctx, tiles, extra)
                loss = criterion(logits, labels)
                loss.backward()
                nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
                optimizer.step()
                train_loss += loss.item() * ctx.size(0)

            scheduler.step()

            model.eval()
            val_logits = []
            val_loss = 0.0
            with torch.no_grad():
                for batch in va_loader:
                    ctx = batch["ctx"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    tiles = batch["tiles"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    extra = batch["extra"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    labels = batch["label"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    logits = model(ctx, tiles, extra)
                    loss = criterion(logits, labels)
                    val_loss += loss.item() * ctx.size(0)
                    val_logits.append(logits.float().cpu())

            val_probs = torch.cat(val_logits).softmax(dim=1).detach().numpy()
            val_pred = val_probs.argmax(1)
            val_acc = (val_pred == train_labels[va_idx]).mean()
            val_thr, val_nm, _ = sweep_threshold(val_probs, train_labels[va_idx])
            monitor = val_nm + 0.05 * val_acc

            if monitor > best_metric:
                best_metric = monitor
                best_state = copy.deepcopy(model.state_dict())
                best_thr = val_thr
                best_acc = float(val_acc)
                patience_left = CFG.patience
            else:
                patience_left -= 1

            if epoch == 1 or epoch % 6 == 0 or patience_left == 0:
                print(
                    f"E{epoch:02d} | "
                    f"TrLoss={train_loss / len(tr_ds):.4f} | "
                    f"VaLoss={val_loss / len(va_ds):.4f} | "
                    f"VaAcc={val_acc:.3f} | "
                    f"VaNM={val_nm:.3f} | "
                    f"Thr={val_thr:.3f}"
                )

            if patience_left == 0:
                print("  ⏹️ Early stopping")
                break

        model.load_state_dict(best_state)
        model.eval()

        with torch.no_grad():
            va_logits = []
            for batch in va_loader:
                ctx = batch["ctx"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                tiles = batch["tiles"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                extra = batch["extra"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                va_logits.append(model(ctx, tiles, extra).float().cpu())
            va_probs = torch.cat(va_logits).softmax(dim=1).detach().numpy()

            te_logits = []
            for batch in te_loader:
                ctx = batch["ctx"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                tiles = batch["tiles"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                extra = batch["extra"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                te_logits.append(model(ctx, tiles, extra).float().cpu())
            te_probs = torch.cat(te_logits).softmax(dim=1).detach().numpy()

        oof_probs[va_idx] += va_probs / len(CFG.seeds)
        test_probs_all.append(te_probs)

        fold_records.append(
            {
                "seed": seed,
                "fold": fold,
                "best_val_acc": best_acc,
                "best_val_thr": best_thr,
                "best_val_metric": best_metric,
            }
        )
        print(f"✅ Fold best acc: {best_acc:.4f} | best thr: {best_thr:.3f}")

        del model
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
        gc.collect()

fold_df = pd.DataFrame(fold_records)
fold_df.to_csv(CFG.fold_metrics_path, index=False)

oof_pred = oof_probs.argmax(1)
oof_thr, oof_nm, oof_conf = sweep_threshold(oof_probs, train_labels)
oof_acc = (oof_pred == train_labels).mean()
kept = oof_conf >= oof_thr
kept_acc = (oof_pred[kept] == train_labels[kept]).mean() if kept.any() else 0.0

print(f"\\n{'=' * 68}")
print(f"OOF Accuracy:          {oof_acc:.4f}")
print(f"Best OOF NM score:     {oof_nm:.4f}")
print(f"Best skip threshold:   {oof_thr:.3f}")
print(f"OOF skip rate:         {(~kept).mean():.1%}")
print(f"Accuracy on kept rows: {kept_acc:.4f}")

test_probs = np.mean(test_probs_all, axis=0)
np.savez_compressed(
    CFG.prob_cache,
    oof_probs=oof_probs,
    test_probs=test_probs,
    oof_conf=oof_conf,
    oof_thr=oof_thr,
)
print(f"💾 Saved OOF/test probabilities to {CFG.prob_cache}")
"""
)

extra_files.solution.py:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Train and infer for the 3D Cross Section challenge.

Primary data layout:
  ./dataset/public/train.csv
  ./dataset/public/test.csv

Primary output:
  ./working/submisison.csv
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import gc
import math
import random
import warnings
from contextlib import nullcontext
from pathlib import Path

import albumentations as A
import cv2
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import timm
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from albumentations.pytorch import ToTensorV2
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset


warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")


if hasattr(torch, "set_float32_matmul_precision"):
    torch.set_float32_matmul_precision("high")


class CFG:
    seed = 42
    n_folds = 5
    seeds = [42, 137, 2024]

    region_size = 224
    text_size = 224
    batch_size = 16
    epochs = 36
    patience = 8
    lr = 8e-4
    wd = 0.02
    label_smoothing = 0.05
    feature_noise = 0.015
    num_workers = 2

    device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
    use_amp = torch.cuda.is_available()

    backbones = [
        "convnext_tiny.in12k_ft_in1k",
        "vit_base_patch16_224",
    ]

    scene_box = (20, 410, 25, 807)
    equation_box = (420, 522, 18, 814)
    solid_equation_box = (423, 468, 18, 814)
    plane_equation_box = (468, 520, 18, 814)
    tile_boxes = [
        (535, 665, 38, 275),
        (535, 665, 283, 520),
        (535, 665, 528, 765),
        (672, 802, 38, 275),
        (672, 802, 283, 520),
        (672, 802, 528, 765),
    ]
    tile_inner = dict(top=22, bottom=10, left=10, right=10)

    work_dir = Path("./working")
    feature_cache = work_dir / "solution_rank_features_v2_textsplit.npz"
    prob_cache = work_dir / "solution_rank_probs_v2_textsplit.npz"
    output_path = work_dir / "submisison.csv"
    output_alias = work_dir / "submission.csv"
    fold_metrics_path = work_dir / "fold_metrics.csv"
    oof_report_path = work_dir / "oof_report.csv"


CFG.work_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)


def seed_all(seed: int) -> None:
    random.seed(seed)
    np.random.seed(seed)
    torch.manual_seed(seed)
    if torch.cuda.is_available():
        torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(seed)
    torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic = False
    torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = True


def discover_data_root() -> Path:
    candidates = [
        Path("./dataset/public"),
        Path("./dataset"),
        Path("."),
    ]
    for root in candidates:
        if (root / "train.csv").exists() and (root / "test.csv").exists():
            return root
    raise FileNotFoundError(
        "Could not find dataset root. Expected ./dataset/public/ or compatible fallback."
    )


def load_rgb(path: str | Path) -> np.ndarray:
    img = cv2.imread(str(path))
    if img is None:
        raise FileNotFoundError(path)
    return cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)


def crop_box(img: np.ndarray, box: tuple[int, int, int, int]) -> np.ndarray:
    y1, y2, x1, x2 = box
    return img[y1:y2, x1:x2]


def extract_tiles(img: np.ndarray, inner: bool = True) -> list[np.ndarray]:
    out: list[np.ndarray] = []
    for box in CFG.tile_boxes:
        tile = crop_box(img, box)
        if inner:
            m = CFG.tile_inner
            tile = tile[
                m["top"] : tile.shape[0] - m["bottom"],
                m["left"] : tile.shape[1] - m["right"],
            ]
        out.append(tile)
    return out


def build_transform(size: int = 224) -> A.Compose:
    return A.Compose(
        [
            A.LongestMaxSize(max_size=size),
            A.PadIfNeeded(
                min_height=size,
                min_width=size,
                border_mode=cv2.BORDER_CONSTANT,
                value=(255, 255, 255),
            ),
            A.Normalize(mean=(0.485, 0.456, 0.406), std=(0.229, 0.224, 0.225)),
            ToTensorV2(),
        ]
    )


def build_text_transform(size: int = 224) -> A.Compose:
    return A.Compose(
        [
            A.Resize(height=size, width=size),
            A.Normalize(mean=(0.485, 0.456, 0.406), std=(0.229, 0.224, 0.225)),
            ToTensorV2(),
        ]
    )


def tile_mask(tile: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    hsv = cv2.cvtColor(tile, cv2.COLOR_RGB2HSV)
    sat = hsv[..., 1].astype(np.float32)
    val = hsv[..., 2].astype(np.float32)
    mask = ((sat > 18) & (val < 252)).astype(np.uint8)
    kernel3 = np.ones((3, 3), np.uint8)
    kernel5 = np.ones((5, 5), np.uint8)
    mask = cv2.morphologyEx(mask, cv2.MORPH_OPEN, kernel3)
    mask = cv2.morphologyEx(mask, cv2.MORPH_CLOSE, kernel5)
    return mask


def contour_holes(mask: np.ndarray) -> int:
    contours, hierarchy = cv2.findContours(mask, cv2.RETR_CCOMP, cv2.CHAIN_APPROX_SIMPLE)
    if hierarchy is None:
        return 0
    hierarchy = hierarchy[0]
    holes = 0
    for i in range(len(contours)):
        if hierarchy[i][3] != -1:
            holes += 1
    return holes


def tile_geom_features(tile: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    mask = tile_mask(tile)
    h, w = mask.shape
    area = float(mask.sum())
    area_ratio = area / (h * w + 1e-6)

    ys, xs = np.where(mask > 0)
    if len(xs) == 0:
        return np.zeros(12, dtype=np.float32)

    x1, x2 = xs.min(), xs.max()
    y1, y2 = ys.min(), ys.max()
    bw = float(max(1, x2 - x1 + 1))
    bh = float(max(1, y2 - y1 + 1))
    bbox_area = bw * bh
    aspect = bw / bh
    extent = area / (bbox_area + 1e-6)

    contours, _ = cv2.findContours(mask, cv2.RETR_EXTERNAL, cv2.CHAIN_APPROX_SIMPLE)
    cnt = max(contours, key=cv2.contourArea) if contours else None
    perimeter = float(cv2.arcLength(cnt, True)) if cnt is not None else 0.0
    hull_area = float(cv2.contourArea(cv2.convexHull(cnt))) if cnt is not None else 0.0
    solidity = area / (hull_area + 1e-6) if hull_area > 0 else 0.0
    circularity = 4.0 * math.pi * area / (perimeter * perimeter + 1e-6) if perimeter > 0 else 0.0
    components, _ = cv2.connectedComponents(mask)
    holes = contour_holes(mask)

    norm_x = xs.astype(np.float32) / max(1.0, w - 1)
    norm_y = ys.astype(np.float32) / max(1.0, h - 1)
    cx = float(norm_x.mean())
    cy = float(norm_y.mean())
    sx = float(norm_x.std())
    sy = float(norm_y.std())

    return np.array(
        [
            area_ratio,
            aspect,
            extent,
            solidity,
            circularity,
            float(components - 1),
            float(holes),
            cx,
            cy,
            sx,
            sy,
            perimeter / (2 * (h + w) + 1e-6),
        ],
        dtype=np.float32,
    )


def confidence_from_probs(probs: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    sorted_probs = np.sort(probs, axis=1)
    top1 = sorted_probs[:, -1]
    top2 = sorted_probs[:, -2]
    margin = top1 - top2
    entropy = -(probs * np.log(probs + 1e-12)).sum(axis=1) / math.log(probs.shape[1])
    return 0.60 * top1 + 0.25 * margin + 0.15 * (1.0 - entropy)


def neg_mark_score(preds: np.ndarray, labels: np.ndarray, confs: np.ndarray, thr: float) -> float:
    score = 0.0
    for pred, label, conf in zip(preds, labels, confs):
        if conf < thr:
            score += 0.0
        elif pred == label:
            score += 1.0
        else:
            score -= 0.25
    return score / len(preds)


def sweep_threshold(
    probs: np.ndarray,
    labels: np.ndarray,
    low: float = 0.15,
    high: float = 0.80,
    step: float = 0.005,
) -> tuple[float, float, np.ndarray]:
    preds = probs.argmax(1)
    confs = confidence_from_probs(probs)
    best_thr, best_nm = low, -1e9
    for thr in np.arange(low, high + 1e-9, step):
        nm = neg_mark_score(preds, labels, confs, float(thr))
        if nm > best_nm:
            best_nm = nm
            best_thr = float(thr)
    return best_thr, float(best_nm), confs


def autocast_context():
    if CFG.use_amp:
        return torch.autocast(device_type="cuda", dtype=torch.float16)
    return nullcontext()


def fit_standardizer(
    ctx: np.ndarray, tiles: np.ndarray, extra: np.ndarray
) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    tile_flat = tiles.reshape(-1, tiles.shape[-1])
    extra_flat = extra.reshape(-1, extra.shape[-1])
    return {
        "ctx_mean": ctx.mean(axis=0),
        "ctx_std": ctx.std(axis=0) + 1e-6,
        "tile_mean": tile_flat.mean(axis=0),
        "tile_std": tile_flat.std(axis=0) + 1e-6,
        "extra_mean": extra_flat.mean(axis=0),
        "extra_std": extra_flat.std(axis=0) + 1e-6,
    }


def apply_standardizer(
    ctx: np.ndarray,
    tiles: np.ndarray,
    extra: np.ndarray,
    stats: dict[str, np.ndarray],
) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
    ctx = (ctx - stats["ctx_mean"]) / stats["ctx_std"]
    tiles = (tiles - stats["tile_mean"]) / stats["tile_std"]
    extra = (extra - stats["extra_mean"]) / stats["extra_std"]
    return ctx.astype(np.float32), tiles.astype(np.float32), extra.astype(np.float32)


class RankingDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(
        self,
        ctx: np.ndarray,
        tiles: np.ndarray,
        extra: np.ndarray,
        labels: np.ndarray | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.ctx = torch.tensor(ctx, dtype=torch.float32)
        self.tiles = torch.tensor(tiles, dtype=torch.float32)
        self.extra = torch.tensor(extra, dtype=torch.float32)
        self.labels = None if labels is None else torch.tensor(labels, dtype=torch.long)

    def __len__(self) -> int:
        return len(self.ctx)

    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
        item = {
            "ctx": self.ctx[idx],
            "tiles": self.tiles[idx],
            "extra": self.extra[idx],
        }
        if self.labels is not None:
            item["label"] = self.labels[idx]
        return item


class TileRanker(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, ctx_dim: int, tile_dim: int, extra_dim: int, hidden: int = 256, dropout: float = 0.25) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        self.ctx_proj = nn.Sequential(
            nn.LayerNorm(ctx_dim),
            nn.Linear(ctx_dim, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
        )
        self.tile_proj = nn.Sequential(
            nn.LayerNorm(tile_dim),
            nn.Linear(tile_dim, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
        )
        self.extra_proj = nn.Sequential(
            nn.LayerNorm(extra_dim),
            nn.Linear(extra_dim, hidden // 2),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout * 0.5),
        )
        self.scorer = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(hidden * 4 + hidden // 2, hidden),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden, 96),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout * 0.5),
            nn.Linear(96, 1),
        )

    def forward(self, ctx: torch.Tensor, tiles: torch.Tensor, extra: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        ctx_h = self.ctx_proj(ctx)
        tile_h = self.tile_proj(tiles)
        extra_h = self.extra_proj(extra)

        ctx_r = ctx_h.unsqueeze(1).expand(-1, tiles.size(1), -1)
        pair = torch.cat(
            [
                ctx_r,
                tile_h,
                torch.abs(ctx_r - tile_h),
                ctx_r * tile_h,
                extra_h,
            ],
            dim=-1,
        )
        return self.scorer(pair).squeeze(-1)


def build_extras(context_arr: np.ndarray, tile_arr: np.ndarray, geom_arr: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    ctx_dim = context_arr.shape[1] // 3
    scene_feat = context_arr[:, :ctx_dim]
    solid_feat = context_arr[:, ctx_dim : 2 * ctx_dim]
    plane_feat = context_arr[:, 2 * ctx_dim :]

    def normalize(x: np.ndarray, axis: int = -1) -> np.ndarray:
        norm = np.linalg.norm(x, axis=axis, keepdims=True) + 1e-6
        return x / norm

    scene_n = normalize(scene_feat)
    solid_n = normalize(solid_feat)
    plane_n = normalize(plane_feat)
    tiles_n = normalize(tile_arr, axis=2)

    scene_cos = np.sum(tiles_n * scene_n[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    solid_cos = np.sum(tiles_n * solid_n[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    plane_cos = np.sum(tiles_n * plane_n[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    scene_l2 = np.linalg.norm(tile_arr - scene_feat[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    solid_l2 = np.linalg.norm(tile_arr - solid_feat[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)
    plane_l2 = np.linalg.norm(tile_arr - plane_feat[:, None, :], axis=2, keepdims=True)

    scene_solid_cos = np.sum(scene_n * solid_n, axis=1, keepdims=True)[:, None, :]
    scene_plane_cos = np.sum(scene_n * plane_n, axis=1, keepdims=True)[:, None, :]
    solid_plane_cos = np.sum(solid_n * plane_n, axis=1, keepdims=True)[:, None, :]
    scene_solid_cos = np.repeat(scene_solid_cos, 6, axis=1)
    scene_plane_cos = np.repeat(scene_plane_cos, 6, axis=1)
    solid_plane_cos = np.repeat(solid_plane_cos, 6, axis=1)

    tile_sim = np.matmul(tiles_n, np.transpose(tiles_n, (0, 2, 1)))
    mean_other = []
    max_other = []
    min_other = []
    for i in range(6):
        others = [j for j in range(6) if j != i]
        mean_other.append(tile_sim[:, i, others].mean(axis=1, keepdims=True))
        max_other.append(tile_sim[:, i, others].max(axis=1, keepdims=True))
        min_other.append(tile_sim[:, i, others].min(axis=1, keepdims=True))

    mean_other = np.stack(mean_other, axis=1)
    max_other = np.stack(max_other, axis=1)
    min_other = np.stack(min_other, axis=1)

    return np.concatenate(
        [
            scene_cos,
            solid_cos,
            plane_cos,
            scene_l2,
            solid_l2,
            plane_l2,
            scene_solid_cos,
            scene_plane_cos,
            solid_plane_cos,
            mean_other,
            max_other,
            min_other,
            geom_arr,
        ],
        axis=2,
    ).astype(np.float32)


@torch.no_grad()
def extract_all_features(
    train_df: pd.DataFrame,
    test_df: pd.DataFrame,
) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    if CFG.feature_cache.exists():
        cache = np.load(CFG.feature_cache, allow_pickle=True)
        cached_train_ids = cache.get("train_ids")
        cached_test_ids = cache.get("test_ids")
        if cached_train_ids is not None and cached_test_ids is not None:
            if np.array_equal(cached_train_ids, train_df["id"].values) and np.array_equal(
                cached_test_ids, test_df["id"].values
            ):
                print(f"Loaded cached features from {CFG.feature_cache}")
                return {
                    "train_context": cache["train_context"],
                    "test_context": cache["test_context"],
                    "train_tiles": cache["train_tiles"],
                    "test_tiles": cache["test_tiles"],
                    "train_extra": cache["train_extra"],
                    "test_extra": cache["test_extra"],
                }
        print("Feature cache exists but ids do not match current dataset. Recomputing.")

    vision_transform = build_transform(CFG.region_size)
    text_transform = build_text_transform(CFG.text_size)
    split_outputs: dict[str, list[np.ndarray]] = {
        "train_context_parts": [],
        "test_context_parts": [],
        "train_tile_parts": [],
        "test_tile_parts": [],
    }
    train_geom: list[np.ndarray] = []
    test_geom: list[np.ndarray] = []

    for bname in CFG.backbones:
        print(f"\nBackbone: {bname}")
        model = timm.create_model(bname, pretrained=True, num_classes=0).to(CFG.device).eval()
        feat_dim = model.num_features

        for split_name, frame, geom_store in [
            ("train", train_df, train_geom),
            ("test", test_df, test_geom),
        ]:
            context_feat = np.zeros((len(frame), feat_dim * 3), dtype=np.float32)
            tile_feat = np.zeros((len(frame), 6, feat_dim), dtype=np.float32)

            if len(geom_store) == 0:
                geom_feat = np.zeros((len(frame), 6, 12), dtype=np.float32)
            else:
                geom_feat = geom_store[0]

            for idx, row in enumerate(frame.itertuples(index=False)):
                img = load_rgb(row.image_path)
                scene = crop_box(img, CFG.scene_box)
                solid_equation = crop_box(img, CFG.solid_equation_box)
                plane_equation = crop_box(img, CFG.plane_equation_box)
                tiles = extract_tiles(img, inner=True)

                crops = [
                    vision_transform(image=scene)["image"],
                    text_transform(image=solid_equation)["image"],
                    text_transform(image=plane_equation)["image"],
                ]
                crops.extend(vision_transform(image=tile)["image"] for tile in tiles)
                batch = torch.stack(crops).to(CFG.device)

                with autocast_context():
                    feats = model(batch).detach().float().cpu().numpy()

                context_feat[idx] = np.concatenate([feats[0], feats[1], feats[2]], axis=0)
                tile_feat[idx] = feats[3:]

                if len(geom_store) == 0:
                    for tile_idx, tile in enumerate(tiles):
                        geom_feat[idx, tile_idx] = tile_geom_features(tile)

            if split_name == "train":
                split_outputs["train_context_parts"].append(context_feat)
                split_outputs["train_tile_parts"].append(tile_feat)
            else:
                split_outputs["test_context_parts"].append(context_feat)
                split_outputs["test_tile_parts"].append(tile_feat)

            if len(geom_store) == 0:
                geom_store.append(geom_feat)

        del model
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
        gc.collect()

    train_context = np.concatenate(split_outputs["train_context_parts"], axis=1)
    test_context = np.concatenate(split_outputs["test_context_parts"], axis=1)
    train_tiles = np.concatenate(split_outputs["train_tile_parts"], axis=2)
    test_tiles = np.concatenate(split_outputs["test_tile_parts"], axis=2)

    train_extra = build_extras(train_context, train_tiles, train_geom[0])
    test_extra = build_extras(test_context, test_tiles, test_geom[0])

    np.savez_compressed(
        CFG.feature_cache,
        train_ids=train_df["id"].values,
        test_ids=test_df["id"].values,
        train_context=train_context,
        test_context=test_context,
        train_tiles=train_tiles,
        test_tiles=test_tiles,
        train_extra=train_extra,
        test_extra=test_extra,
    )
    print(f"Saved feature cache to {CFG.feature_cache}")

    return {
        "train_context": train_context,
        "test_context": test_context,
        "train_tiles": train_tiles,
        "test_tiles": test_tiles,
        "train_extra": train_extra,
        "test_extra": test_extra,
    }


def train_and_predict(
    train_df: pd.DataFrame,
    test_df: pd.DataFrame,
    train_labels: np.ndarray,
    train_context: np.ndarray,
    test_context: np.ndarray,
    train_tiles: np.ndarray,
    test_tiles: np.ndarray,
    train_extra: np.ndarray,
    test_extra: np.ndarray,
) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, float, float, np.ndarray]:
    ctx_dim = train_context.shape[1]
    tile_dim = train_tiles.shape[2]
    extra_dim = train_extra.shape[2]

    oof_probs = np.zeros((len(train_df), 6), dtype=np.float32)
    test_probs_all: list[np.ndarray] = []
    fold_records: list[dict[str, float | int]] = []

    for seed in CFG.seeds:
        print(f"\n{'#' * 68}")
        print(f"SEED = {seed}")
        print(f"{'#' * 68}")
        seed_all(seed)

        skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=CFG.n_folds, shuffle=True, random_state=seed)

        for fold, (tr_idx, va_idx) in enumerate(skf.split(train_df, train_df["answer"]), start=1):
            print(f"\n--- Fold {fold}/{CFG.n_folds} ---")

            stats = fit_standardizer(
                train_context[tr_idx],
                train_tiles[tr_idx],
                train_extra[tr_idx],
            )

            tr_ctx, tr_tiles, tr_extra = apply_standardizer(
                train_context[tr_idx], train_tiles[tr_idx], train_extra[tr_idx], stats
            )
            va_ctx, va_tiles, va_extra = apply_standardizer(
                train_context[va_idx], train_tiles[va_idx], train_extra[va_idx], stats
            )
            te_ctx, te_tiles, te_extra = apply_standardizer(
                test_context, test_tiles, test_extra, stats
            )

            tr_ds = RankingDataset(tr_ctx, tr_tiles, tr_extra, train_labels[tr_idx])
            va_ds = RankingDataset(va_ctx, va_tiles, va_extra, train_labels[va_idx])
            te_ds = RankingDataset(te_ctx, te_tiles, te_extra, None)

            tr_loader = DataLoader(
                tr_ds,
                batch_size=CFG.batch_size,
                shuffle=True,
                num_workers=CFG.num_workers,
                pin_memory=torch.cuda.is_available(),
            )
            va_loader = DataLoader(
                va_ds,
                batch_size=CFG.batch_size,
                shuffle=False,
                num_workers=CFG.num_workers,
                pin_memory=torch.cuda.is_available(),
            )
            te_loader = DataLoader(
                te_ds,
                batch_size=CFG.batch_size,
                shuffle=False,
                num_workers=CFG.num_workers,
                pin_memory=torch.cuda.is_available(),
            )

            model = TileRanker(ctx_dim, tile_dim, extra_dim).to(CFG.device)
            criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=CFG.label_smoothing)
            optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=CFG.lr, weight_decay=CFG.wd)
            scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=CFG.epochs)

            best_metric = -1e9
            best_state = None
            best_thr = 0.20
            best_acc = 0.0
            patience_left = CFG.patience

            for epoch in range(1, CFG.epochs + 1):
                model.train()
                train_loss = 0.0

                for batch in tr_loader:
                    ctx = batch["ctx"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    tiles = batch["tiles"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    extra = batch["extra"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    labels = batch["label"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)

                    if CFG.feature_noise > 0:
                        ctx = ctx + torch.randn_like(ctx) * CFG.feature_noise
                        tiles = tiles + torch.randn_like(tiles) * CFG.feature_noise
                        extra = extra + torch.randn_like(extra) * (CFG.feature_noise * 0.5)

                    optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
                    logits = model(ctx, tiles, extra)
                    loss = criterion(logits, labels)
                    loss.backward()
                    nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
                    optimizer.step()
                    train_loss += loss.item() * ctx.size(0)

                scheduler.step()

                model.eval()
                val_logits = []
                val_loss = 0.0
                with torch.no_grad():
                    for batch in va_loader:
                        ctx = batch["ctx"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                        tiles = batch["tiles"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                        extra = batch["extra"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                        labels = batch["label"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                        logits = model(ctx, tiles, extra)
                        loss = criterion(logits, labels)
                        val_loss += loss.item() * ctx.size(0)
                        val_logits.append(logits.float().cpu())

                val_probs = torch.cat(val_logits).softmax(dim=1).detach().numpy()
                val_pred = val_probs.argmax(1)
                val_acc = (val_pred == train_labels[va_idx]).mean()
                val_thr, val_nm, _ = sweep_threshold(val_probs, train_labels[va_idx])
                monitor = val_nm + 0.05 * val_acc

                if monitor > best_metric:
                    best_metric = monitor
                    best_state = copy.deepcopy(model.state_dict())
                    best_thr = val_thr
                    best_acc = float(val_acc)
                    patience_left = CFG.patience
                else:
                    patience_left -= 1

                if epoch == 1 or epoch % 6 == 0 or patience_left == 0:
                    print(
                        f"E{epoch:02d} | "
                        f"TrLoss={train_loss / len(tr_ds):.4f} | "
                        f"VaLoss={val_loss / len(va_ds):.4f} | "
                        f"VaAcc={val_acc:.3f} | "
                        f"VaNM={val_nm:.3f} | "
                        f"Thr={val_thr:.3f}"
                    )

                if patience_left == 0:
                    print("  Early stopping")
                    break

            model.load_state_dict(best_state)
            model.eval()

            with torch.no_grad():
                va_logits = []
                for batch in va_loader:
                    ctx = batch["ctx"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    tiles = batch["tiles"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    extra = batch["extra"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    va_logits.append(model(ctx, tiles, extra).float().cpu())
                va_probs = torch.cat(va_logits).softmax(dim=1).detach().numpy()

                te_logits = []
                for batch in te_loader:
                    ctx = batch["ctx"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    tiles = batch["tiles"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    extra = batch["extra"].to(CFG.device, non_blocking=True)
                    te_logits.append(model(ctx, tiles, extra).float().cpu())
                te_probs = torch.cat(te_logits).softmax(dim=1).detach().numpy()

            oof_probs[va_idx] += va_probs / len(CFG.seeds)
            test_probs_all.append(te_probs)

            fold_records.append(
                {
                    "seed": seed,
                    "fold": fold,
                    "best_val_acc": best_acc,
                    "best_val_thr": best_thr,
                    "best_val_metric": best_metric,
                }
            )
            print(f"Fold best acc: {best_acc:.4f} | best thr: {best_thr:.3f}")

            del model
            torch.cuda.empty_cache()
            gc.collect()

    fold_df = pd.DataFrame(fold_records)
    fold_df.to_csv(CFG.fold_metrics_path, index=False)

    oof_pred = oof_probs.argmax(1)
    oof_thr, oof_nm, oof_conf = sweep_threshold(oof_probs, train_labels)
    oof_acc = (oof_pred == train_labels).mean()
    kept = oof_conf >= oof_thr
    kept_acc = (oof_pred[kept] == train_labels[kept]).mean() if kept.any() else 0.0

    print(f"\n{'=' * 68}")
    print(f"OOF Accuracy:          {oof_acc:.4f}")
    print(f"Best OOF NM score:     {oof_nm:.4f}")
    print(f"Best skip threshold:   {oof_thr:.3f}")
    print(f"OOF skip rate:         {(~kept).mean():.1%}")
    print(f"Accuracy on kept rows: {kept_acc:.4f}")

    test_probs = np.mean(test_probs_all, axis=0)
    np.savez_compressed(
        CFG.prob_cache,
        oof_probs=oof_probs,
        test_probs=test_probs,
        oof_conf=oof_conf,
        oof_thr=oof_thr,
    )
    print(f"Saved probability cache to {CFG.prob_cache}")
    return oof_probs, test_probs, oof_thr, oof_nm, oof_conf


def write_submission(
    test_df: pd.DataFrame,
    train_df: pd.DataFrame,
    train_labels: np.ndarray,
    oof_probs: np.ndarray,
    test_probs: np.ndarray,
    oof_thr: float,
    oof_conf: np.ndarray,
) -> None:
    inv_map = {i: c for i, c in enumerate("ABCDEF")}

    test_pred = test_probs.argmax(1)
    test_conf = confidence_from_probs(test_probs)
    answers = [inv_map[int(pred)] if conf >= oof_thr else "-" for pred, conf in zip(test_pred, test_conf)]

    sub = pd.DataFrame({"id": test_df["id"], "answer": answers})
    sub.to_csv(CFG.output_path, index=False)
    sub.to_csv(CFG.output_alias, index=False)

    oof_pred = oof_probs.argmax(1)
    oof_report = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "id": train_df["id"],
            "answer": train_df["answer"],
            "pred": [inv_map[int(x)] for x in oof_pred],
            "conf": oof_conf,
            "skip": oof_conf < oof_thr,
        }
    )
    oof_report.to_csv(CFG.oof_report_path, index=False)

    print("\nTest confidence stats:")
    print(f"  Mean: {test_conf.mean():.3f}")
    print(f"  Min:  {test_conf.min():.3f}")
    print(f"  Max:  {test_conf.max():.3f}")
    print(f"  Skip rate @ {oof_thr:.3f}: {(test_conf < oof_thr).mean():.1%}")

    print("\nSubmission distribution:")
    print(sub["answer"].value_counts().sort_index())
    print(f"\nSaved submission to {CFG.output_path}")
    print(f"Also mirrored to {CFG.output_alias}")


def main() -> None:
    seed_all(CFG.seed)
    data_root = discover_data_root()

    train_df = pd.read_csv(data_root / "train.csv")
    test_df = pd.read_csv(data_root / "test.csv")
    train_df["image_path"] = train_df["image"].map(lambda p: str(data_root / p))
    test_df["image_path"] = test_df["image"].map(lambda p: str(data_root / p))
    train_labels = train_df["answer"].map({c: i for i, c in enumerate("ABCDEF")}).values.astype(np.int64)

    print(f"Data root: {data_root.resolve()}")
    print(f"Device: {CFG.device}")
    if torch.cuda.is_available():
        prop = torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0)
        print(f"GPU: {torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)} | {prop.total_memory / 1e9:.1f} GB")
    print(f"Train rows: {len(train_df)} | Test rows: {len(test_df)}")

    feature_dict = extract_all_features(train_df, test_df)
    print("\nFeature shapes:")
    print("train_context:", feature_dict["train_context"].shape)
    print("test_context: ", feature_dict["test_context"].shape)
    print("train_tiles:  ", feature_dict["train_tiles"].shape)
    print("test_tiles:   ", feature_dict["test_tiles"].shape)
    print("train_extra:  ", feature_dict["train_extra"].shape)
    print("test_extra:   ", feature_dict["test_extra"].shape)

    oof_probs, test_probs, oof_thr, _oof_nm, oof_conf = train_and_predict(
        train_df=train_df,
        test_df=test_df,
        train_labels=train_labels,
        train_context=feature_dict["train_context"],
        test_context=feature_dict["test_context"],
        train_tiles=feature_dict["train_tiles"],
        test_tiles=feature_dict["test_tiles"],
        train_extra=feature_dict["train_extra"],
        test_extra=feature_dict["test_extra"],
    )
    write_submission(
        test_df=test_df,
        train_df=train_df,
        train_labels=train_labels,
        oof_probs=oof_probs,
        test_probs=test_probs,
        oof_thr=oof_thr,
        oof_conf=oof_conf,
    )


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
extra_files.solver_context.md:
# Solver Context

You are working on the challenge `3D Cross Section Silhouette Challenge`.

## Goal
Build a real Machine Learning solution that can generalize to unseen
data. Focus on clean, reusable code and a notebook that can run on
Google Colab with A100 hardware.

## Required outputs
- Main notebook: `/Users/havanhai/shipd/3D Cross Section Silhouette Challenge/notebook.ipynb`
- Working directory for experiments: `/Users/havanhai/shipd/3D Cross Section Silhouette Challenge/working`
- Dataset directory: `/Users/havanhai/shipd/3D Cross Section Silhouette Challenge/dataset`
- Repacked Colab archive: `/Users/havanhai/shipd/3D Cross Section Silhouette Challenge/dataset.zip`

## Challenge description
Overview
3D Cross Section Silhouette Challenge is a 3D-plus-equations reasoning benchmark. Each task is a single 832x832 PNG depicting a geometric solid sliced by a flat cutting plane, with six candidate 2D silhouettes labelled A, B, C, D, E, F shown underneath. Exactly one of the six silhouettes is the true plane-solid cross-section, and the task is to pick that letter.

Twelve solid families are used across the dataset: sphere, ellipsoid, rectangular box, capped cylinder, elliptic paraboloid, triangular prism, capped cone, torus, square pyramid, one-sheet hyperboloid, bicone, and L-shaped prism. Six of them (sphere, ellipsoid, box, cylinder, paraboloid, triangular prism) are in-distribution and appear in training and the in-distribution test split. The other six (cone, torus, square pyramid, hyperboloid, bicone, L-shaped prism) are held out and appear only in the test_novel_solids and test_hard diagnostic splits.

Each solid is rendered at a random 3D orientation (sphere only, which is rotation-invariant, is kept axis-aligned). The cutting plane is always axis-aligned in the world frame: x = k, y = k, or z = k with an integer k. World-frame x, y, z axes pass through the origin; only the axis the plane is aligned with carries integer tick marks and numeric labels, and the plane's own position on that axis is highlighted as a larger orange tick plus a bold number. So the solver reads the plane position directly off the labelled axis.

The printed solid equation is in the solid's local reference frame. The printed plane equation is in the world reference frame. To find the cross-section, the solver must combine the solid's shape (from the equation), its orientation (visible in the 3D render), and the plane's position (from the plane equation and the highlighted axis tick).

Every silhouette is canonicalised with its major axis horizontal. Matching is therefore about the shape of the cross-section, not its orientation in the plane's local frame. The six options span different coarse shape categories (circle, ellipse variants, polygon variants, triangle, annulus, disjoint pair, non-convex), so the tiles are visually distinct at a glance and the correct letter is the one whose shape the cross-section actually has.

The dataset carries 500 raw tasks total, each with a globally-unique (solid_equation, plane_equation) pair. No two tasks share a symbolic configuration.

Challenge Characteristics
Domain: synthetic, procedurally generated 3D-geometry puzzles
Input modality: a single 832x832 RGB PNG per task
Output modality: a single uppercase letter in {A, B, C, D, E, F}
Training data: 100 tasks with images and ground-truth letters
Test data: 400 held-out tasks across three diagnostic splits
Evaluation: negative-marking accuracy, +1.0 correct, -0.25 wrong, 0.0 skip, averaged across the test set
Output vocabulary: 6 answer tokens plus a skip token (empty string, whitespace, or -)
Dataset
Every task is delivered as an 832x832 RGB image with the following layout:

+------------------------------------------------+  
|                                                |  
|          [ 3D isometric render ]               |  
|   solid (blue) at random orientation +         |  
|   cutting plane (light orange, dotted grid)    |  
|   + x / y / z axes, numbered ticks on the      |  
|   plane's axis, orange tick at the plane's k   |  
|                                                |  
|  +------------------------------------------+  |  
|  | Solid (name):  implicit equation         |  |  
|  | Plane:         x = k   /   y = k   /     |  |  
|  |                z = k                     |  |  
|  +------------------------------------------+  |  
|                                                |  
|        Which silhouette is the cross-section?  |  
|   +--------+ +--------+ +--------+             |  
|   |   A    | |   B    | |   C    |             |  
|   +--------+ +--------+ +--------+             |  
|   +--------+ +--------+ +--------+             |  
|   |   D    | |   E    | |   F    |             |  
|   +--------+ +--------+ +--------+             |  
+------------------------------------------------+  
The solid is rendered as a smoothly anti-aliased wireframe with a pastel-blue fill and a dark outline.
The cutting plane is a light semi-transparent orange rectangle with a faint dotted grid overlay, drawn so it passes through the solid without dominating the scene.
The world x axis is drawn in a red tint, y in green, z in blue. Only the axis matched to the cutting plane carries numbered integer tick marks; the plane's own position on that axis is a larger orange tick with a bold number.
The printed solid equation uses Unicode math (superscript 2, square root, set-inclusion).
Every plane equation is of the form x = k, y = k, or z = k with integer k.
Silhouette tiles are smoothly filled pastel-blue shapes with dark outlines, scaled to the same canonical bounding box. Every silhouette has been canonicalised so its major axis lies horizontal.
A single letter (A through F) is the ground-truth answer; exactly one of the six candidates matches the cross-section.
Data Files
File	Description	Columns
train.csv	100 training tasks	id, image, answer
train_images/	100 task PNGs (832x832)	
test.csv	400 held-out test tasks	id, image
test_images/	400 test PNGs	
sample_submission.csv	Submission format template	id, answer
The image column is a relative path of the form train_images/train_0000000.png. Participants may carve their own validation split out of train.csv.

The test set is a shuffled mixture of three diagnostic splits:

test_iid (100 tasks): in-distribution baseline over the six in-distribution solid families.
test_novel_solids (150 tasks): held-out solid families (cone, torus, square pyramid, hyperboloid, bicone, L-prism).
test_hard (150 tasks): a second held-out-solids sample, disjoint from test_novel_solids.
Split labels are not exposed in the public test CSV.

Example Row (from train.csv)
id:      train_0000000  
image:   train_images/train_0000000.png  
answer:  E  
Submission Format
Submit a CSV with exactly the columns id and answer. One row per test task; the id column must match test.csv exactly.

id,answer  
test_000000,A  
test_000001,F  
test_000002,C  
Formatting requirements

Each answer is either a single uppercase letter in {A, B, C, D, E, F} or a skip token.
A skip token is an empty string, whitespace only, or the literal character -. A skip earns exactly zero on that row.
Whitespace is trimmed and the first alphabetic character is uppercased before comparison, so " c " is treated as C.
Any non-skip value whose normalised first alphabetic character is outside {A, B, C, D, E, F} is treated as a skip (zero) on that row.
Evaluation: Negative-Marking Accuracy
Per-row scoring:

+1.00 if the prediction matches the ground-truth letter exactly  
-0.25 if the prediction normalises to any other valid letter (a wrong guess)  
 0.00 if the prediction is a skip (empty, whitespace, or "-")  
The final benchmark score is the mean per-task score over the test set, in the closed interval [-0.25, +1.0]. A perfect submission returns 1.0; blanket wrong guessing returns -0.25; fully skipping returns 0.0. Uniform random guessing over six letters has expected value 1/6 * 1.0 + 5/6 * (-0.25) ~= -0.042, so any strategy that cannot learn a signal above chance is actively punished for guessing and should prefer to skip. The break-even confidence is p = 0.20.

Method Requirements
CROSSECTION is a learning-centric benchmark. The intended solution space is any learned approach that takes the rendered image as input and produces a letter.

Prohibited approaches

Rule-based or hand-coded solutions that OCR the printed equation, reconstruct the solid and plane in a symbolic computer-algebra system, and compute the intersection analytically. The benchmark measures what a learned visual-plus-symbolic pipeline can acquire, not the author's ability to re-implement the generator.
Hardcoded lookup tables indexed by the exact equation strings or rendered mask hashes.
Test-time fine-tuning, retrieval over the test set, or per-task adaptation using the test labels.
Allowed approaches

Any learned image-to-label pipeline, including end-to-end CNN or ViT classifiers, image-to-sequence models that emit the letter as a single token, two-stage detect-then-classify systems, vision-language models prompted with the image and a natural-language question, or ensembles of any of the above.
Data augmentation (colour jitter, crops within the layout), auxiliary objectives, or multi-head training that predicts the letter alongside other structural labels, provided only the supplied training data is used to fit parameters.
OCR on the printed equations, provided the OCR model itself is either off-the-shelf or trained only on the supplied training data and is not chained to a hand-written symbolic solver.
 

Expected Output
Output to ./working/submission.csv

## Solver policy

## Execution guidance
- Inspect the dataset structure before picking a model family.
- Prefer robust baselines first, then iterate with stronger ML methods.

- Keep artifacts and experiment outputs inside `working/`.
